# Transcrição: 5. Close

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, in this segment I'm going to talk about how to close for a future presentation. Consider this. You just called somebody cold. You did a great greeting. You dropped a monster claim. You collected some quick fact finding. There's interest. This guy's actually interested. You're like, oh my God, what am I going to do here? But you don't have enough time to move into a full presentation. You haven't gotten into the numbers yet. Okay, or maybe you did. I can increase your sales 15% or you the decision maker. Yes, I am. But I don't have time to talk about it right now, Grant. Can you call me back at a later date? What we're going to do now is we're going to figure out how to actually close on a real solid time to do a presentation at a future date. This is going to happen to you more often than not. Certainly it's going to happen to you more often than being hung up on. And certainly it's going to happen to you more often than somebody saying, yeah, here's a credit card, man. Let's buy something right now. You understand? So on this cold call, you've greeted, you drop your hooks, you created interest, you've done an awesome job, you have the decision maker or the person of influence, and you found out that there might be two other people that need to be on this call. You did a great job. Now what are you going to close on? You're going to close on one of two things now. You're not going to close on the money. You're going to close on a future presentation and solidify a real time and appointment. Number two, you're going to solidify an appointment time, a real time where they will take you seriously. Now, you can always ask for a presentation right now, but in this case, in this example I've given you, the customer said, I can't do it right now. I need two other people involved. Good. But you still could ask for it. Let me ask you this. Let me ask you this. Is there any chance if it only took 12 minutes you could grab those two people right now while I have you? Save you the time of doing it in the future. I could ask for it. Don't ask. But know that a solid appointment is a big win for everybody. I'd rather have the appointment right now. Trust me. I'd rather have it right this second rather than something in the future. That's just me. Now when the prospect gives you the option for a presentation at a future time, like, yeah, Grant, call me next Tuesday. That is not an appointment. You understand? Let me think about it. ## [03:00 - 04:13]

or an influencer on a cold call that wasn't expecting it. That's not going to set for an appointment. When closing an appointment, remember this. You're doing these people a favor. Your product can solve their problem. Push for today. Push for later today. Or, if you can't get that, okay, great. How about tomorrow? How about the next day? How about the third day? Right? Tie this guy down. Once he says yes, I can do that. Or I need to check with them. I'll hold. Look, I have a product that can solve your problems. Okay? That can solve your problems. I'm telling you.
